journal_entry.description: Employee meal provided during overtime as employee benefit
journal_entry.rationale: The transaction is classified as an employee benefit expense. Since the meal was provided to employees working overtime as a benefit, it is recorded as Employee Benefits Expense (or Meals and Entertainment Expense depending on chart of accounts). The corporate credit card creates a liability (Credit Card Payable). The meal is exempt from GST/HST under Canadian tax rules as it qualifies as an employer-provided meal benefit (taxable benefit to employees for income tax purposes, but not subject to GST/HST collection). Debit tuple (0,0,1,0,0,0) indicates one expense increase line. Credit tuple (1,0,0,0,0,0) indicates one liability increase line.

output.reason: The meal provided to employees working overtime as an employee benefit is an employee benefit expense. This increases the expense account, which requires a debit. The debit side records: 1 expense increase (employee benefits/meals expense).

debit_classifier.reason: The meal provided to employees working overtime as an employee benefit is an employee benefit expense. This increases the expense account, which requires a debit. The debit side records: 1 expense increase (employee benefits/meals expense).

output.reason: The corporate credit card balance increases (liability increase) when the meal is charged to it. The credit side records the obligation owed to the credit card company.

credit_classifier.reason: The corporate credit card balance increases (liability increase) when the meal is charged to it. The credit side records the obligation owed to the credit card company.
